runner: manual: cross-compile gen_fixture (NDK x86_64-linux-android24-clang) -> adb push -> adb shell ./gen_fixture --self-test; full vulkan_verify fixture suite cross-compiled and run for SwiftShader diagnostic (software ICD, NOT recordable hardware evidence per fail-closed rule)
sourceOfTruth: Runtime-ready for the CPU backend means the in-tree C reference path (the same arithmetic the fork CPU ops dispatch to) executes correctly on a real Android x86_64 ELF when run on a Cuttlefish (cvd) virtual device under KVM. gen_fixture --self-test exercises turbo3 + turbo4 + turbo3_tcq + qjl + polar + polar+QJL-residual + tbq3_0/tbq4_0 V-cache round-trip + fused-attn QJL-K -> softmax -> V-mix parity at fp32 precision. Scores match the host-built reference bit-for-bit (-2.501480 / -23.721790 / -4.822659 / 3.696591 / -1.994053 / -1.438744). The fork-built llama-server cross-binary loads on the cvd and exposes the full Eliza-1 KV-cache-type whitelist (tbq3_0, tbq4_0, qjl1_256, q4_polar, tbq3_tcq).

host.hypervisor: KVM (host: Ubuntu 24.04, kernel 6.17, /dev/kvm crw-rw---- root kvm; run user in kvm + cvdnetwork groups)

requirements.verifyHarness: packages/inference/verify/gen_fixture.c + verify/qjl_polar_ref.c + reference/turbo_kernels.c cross-compiled with the NDK clang as a static x86_64-linux-android24 ELF; pushed to /data/local/tmp/eliza-x86_64-verify and run via adb shell

target_artifact.deviceCheck: LD_LIBRARY_PATH=. ./llama-server --help loads on cvd-1 and prints the full Eliza-1 KV-cache-type whitelist (tbq3_0, tbq4_0, qjl1_256, q4_polar, tbq3_tcq) and the fork version banner 'version: 4 (536ff214) built with Clang 20.0.0 for Android x86_64'.
target_artifact.loaderRequirement: libomp.so from the NDK clang runtime ($NDK/toolchains/llvm/prebuilt/linux-x86_64/lib/clang/20/lib/linux/x86_64/libomp.so) must be co-located with libggml-cpu.so; tracked in install-dir alongside the other sidecars. This is the same dependency as on the host build.

mtp.note: MTP speculative decode is a server-side execution mode (not a kernel correctness check). The fork llama-server + llama-speculative-simple cross-binaries are present on the cvd ELF; a runtime smoke against a staged Eliza-1 GGUF (in-app or pushed via adb) is the unblocking step. Not part of the kernel-contract C reference.

caveats: Real, finite, deterministic CPU reference scores — bit-identical to the host build — proven on a Cuttlefish (cvd) virtual device running an x86_64 Android image under KVM. cvd CPU is host CPU passthrough (Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX with AVX2 + AVX-VNNI), so this is the right ABI and the right SIMD baseline for ChromeOS x86_64 + Android-emulator + Cuttlefish CI use-cases.

This evidence covers C reference parity (kernel-contract.json kernels.*.runtimeStatus.cpu for turbo3/turbo4/turbo3_tcq/qjl/polar) on the Android x86_64 ABI; it does NOT cover a full MTP spec-decode end-to-end smoke against a staged Eliza-1 GGUF. The next gate (chat-completion against a staged model in the privileged APK) is documented in PLATFORM_MATRIX.md and remains 'needs-hardware' until aosp/build-aosp.mjs --launch stages a model.

android-x86_64-vulkan on cvd is SwiftShader (LLVM 16.0.0; vendor 0x1ae0 = Google software rasterizer). All 8 vulkan_verify cases (turbo3, turbo4, turbo3_tcq, qjl, polar x2, polar_preht x2) PASS on the cvd SwiftShader path with max diff < 1e-5 — recorded as DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY, NOT runtime-ready, per the kernel-contract fail-closed software-ICD rule. Real Android x86_64 Vulkan graph dispatch needs a ChromeOS x86_64 GPU silicon (Adreno/Mali under ARCVM) or a passed-through host GPU via crosvm gfxstream — not exercised here.
